text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer. Readable code is easy to understand, which helps you and others work with it later. Using clear naming conventions is a great start—choose variable and function names that describe their purpose, like `calculateTotal()` instead of `doStuff()`. Keep your functions short and focused on one task; this makes them reusable and easier to test. When your code follows these practices, it becomes more maintainable over time. A well-structured project saves hours of debugging and allows new team members to contribute quickly. Remember, write code for humans first, computers second.
